title: Hometown / Nantong low-altitude loop
visible_surface: low streets, repeated evening coordinates, phone camera
signal_coverage: GPS repeated across several iPhone files
one_fact: The files cluster tightly around low-elevation Nantong/Tongzhou coordinates rather than behaving like destination photos.
narrative_note: This is the hometown line as ordinary return across 2025 and early 2026: low coordinates, almost no altitude, no need to perform as a landmark.
source_note: Nantong official regional material places Tongzhou on the north side of the Yangtze River Delta with Yellow Sea adjacency.

calendar_events.label: CH03 HOMETOWN: Nantong phone-photo cluster begins

title: Hometown / no-coordinate stills
visible_surface: stills with no embedded coordinates, nearby life record
one_fact: All three files carry time and device metadata, but no usable GPS field.
narrative_note: The missing coordinate is useful: it lets the hometown chapter feel less like evidence and more like living nearby.

title: Hometown / Huai'an canal evening
visible_surface: flat waterway city, early evening, low altitude
signal_coverage: GPS matched across four iPhone files
one_fact: The Huai'an group stays around 33.5569, 119.0439, almost level with the plain.
narrative_note: This should read as a remembered everyday city, not a travel trophy: canal, flatness, evening, return.
source_note: Recent state-media reporting describes Huai'an's 125-kilometer Grand Canal corridor as a mix of heritage, ecology and livability.

calendar_events.label: CH03 HOMETOWN: Huai'an evening GPS cluster

title: Hometown / Changshu single coordinate
visible_surface: one coordinate, Yangtze Delta edge, quiet insertion
one_fact: This is a single-file marker, so it should stay light in search rather than become a full chapter explanation.
narrative_note: Changshu works best as a small hometown-adjacent note: one visible pin, no heavy symbolism.
source_note: Changshu is positioned in the Yangtze River Delta and tied administratively to Suzhou.

title: Ascent / Gansu-Qinghai route additions
visible_surface: road, mountain pass, phone altitude, thin-air archive
one_fact: The added files extend the old ascent line with altitudes from roughly 2450m to 2824m.
narrative_note: This is still a route chapter: altitude is useful as a small chart and rhythm, not as a grand spiritual proof.

calendar_events.label: CH04 ASCENT: added MOV GPS route points

title: Ascent / Mount Tai night climb
visible_surface: night climb, stair route, summit-morning altitude
signal_coverage: GPS altitude rises from about 504m to 1508m
one_fact: The photo metadata gives a visible climb curve: 503.8m at night, then around 1500m after sunrise.
narrative_note: 泰山 should support the ascent chapter with a small bottom-right altitude graph, almost like a held breath.
source_note: UNESCO describes Mount Taishan as a sacred mountain with a long imperial-cult history and cultural landscape.

calendar_events.label: CH04 ASCENT: Mt Tai night climb begins
CH04 ASCENT: Mt Tai morning altitude points

title: Brisbane / Victoria-Melbourne extension
visible_surface: Australian extension, High Country road, Melbourne river, Queensland return
signal_coverage: GPS route across four iPhone files
one_fact: The 2026 Australian group moves from Victoria High Country coordinates into central Melbourne, then returns toward Queensland.
narrative_note: Keep this inside Brisbane as an extension of southern-hemisphere living, not as a new epic chapter.
source_note: Visit Victoria frames the Great Alpine Road as Wangaratta-to-Metung via Bright, Mount Hotham and diverse high-country landscapes; Melbourne Water describes the Yarra running through Melbourne to Port Phillip Bay.

calendar_events.label: CH05 BRISBANE: Victoria High Country extension

title: Brisbane / daily coordinates and return
visible_surface: river city, commute coordinates, subtropical light, May return clips
one_fact: The Brisbane additions repeatedly sit near low river-city coordinates from August to November 2025, then return to Brisbane/Morningside/Balmoral coordinates on 2026-05-14.
narrative_note: This should explain the Brisbane chapter as routine and light direction: ferry, river, commute, sun, and return to Queensland after the April 2026 Victoria extension.
source_note: Brisbane City Council describes Brisbane as a subtropical river city on the coastal plain of South East Queensland.

calendar_events.label: CH05 BRISBANE: daily coordinate series begins

title: Afterimage / small-body and monochrome fragments
camera: small color body / monochrome digital body
one_fact: The AFTERIMAGE set is defined less by place than by small color fragments, monochrome files, and missing GPS.
narrative_note: This should stay quiet: a chapter after looking, after color, after the map has stopped helping.
